rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF RAYMOND J. MARTELLI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #7, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/17/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Proof of additional bond must be filed before the accounting can be approved. (2) Please file a copy of the seller's closing statement for the Palm Springs property sold on 5/6/22. (3) For what legal services was Michael G. Kasolas paid on 5/5/22? (4) For what notary fee did the conservator reimburse himself on 12/11/20? (5) Was there an order authorizing the allowance to the conservatee? (6) Please explain the "book-entry shares" of Toyota Motor Corporation. The estate did not own any shares of this corporation. (7) 50,000 shares of Milpitas CA Uni Sch Dist 4.0% 08/01/34 were sold for the same carry value as the 15,000 shares on hand at the beginning of this accounting period? (8) The bank statement for First Federal is for the period ending 12/31/21, not 11/30/22. (9) It would have been helpful if Schedule G had shown the total number of shares of reinvested dividends... Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 5/8/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Conservator's prior actions have no bearing on current fees requested.)

[CRC 2.115.] Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SHEPPARD M. LEVINE
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1226-1228 26th Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94122)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) No inventory and appraisal supporting the value listed at item 3b of the petition is on file. 3) No reappraisal supporting the value listed at item 3c of the petition is on file (although no reappraisal is required if the inventory and appraisal value is $1,655,000 as alleged, as the decedent died within one year before this hearing). 4) The reported sale was made (offer/bid accepted and returned to Court for confirmation) on 02/16/2023, before notice of sale was first published on 02/24/2023, and before the 03/17/2023 deadline specified in the published notice for submitting offers/bids under Probate Code section 10304(a)(2), and thus failed to comply with section 10306(a) and LRSF 14.31B. 5) The report of sale and petition identified one of the buyers as "Lauren Wayin Yee", but the proof of service of notice on file identified her as "Lauren Wayin Lee". 6) The published notice stated that transfer taxes and recording fees must be paid by the buyers, but according to Attachment 4f, the seller is responsible for such transfer taxes and fees. See section 10307 and LRSF 14.31(C). 7) Has Compass agreed to waive a statutory partial commission in the event of a successful overbidder represented by a different broker? See section 10165(c)(3). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MORI IWANAGA 2015 REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST DATED APRIL 29, 2015
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trustee And Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/17/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) This does not appear to be the proper venue. The successor trustee resides in San Mateo. The asset at issue does not appear to be real property; it is shares in a corporation. (2) The verification of this amended petition refers to the incorrect pleading. (3) No courtesy copy of any proof of service has been provided [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] Two proofs of service were reviewed online in hopes of not having to continue this again, but notice is defective. The proof of service filed on 2/23/23 shows that notice was mailed on 2/23/22 (the wrong year, and the amended petition was not e-filed until 3/2/23, when it was first available for viewing on the Court's website), not mailed to Margaret Lockhart, and did not include the language regarding procedures for appearing remotely. The proof of service filed on 3/14/23 shows that notice with the language regarding procedures for appearing remotely was mailed on 3/13/23, which was not at least 30 days before this hearing, and still not given to Margaret Lockhart. If the amended petition is not withdrawn or dismissed, courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 5/8/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (No order has been entered yet; the proposed order should not be an "Amended Order.") When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.]

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: MICHAEL N. FINN TRUST UTD 1/14/2003
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (814 Grove Street, San Francisco, Ca 94117)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The examiner did not review the petition [DE-260] as petitioner did not present any authority under which the court can confirm this sale as presented, specifically when the trust has specific provisions instructing the trustee needs to hold the asset on behalf of Mr. Russell Zeller. Also, petition failed to meet statutory requirements. Petition is completely silent regarding the distribution provision of the trust itself. Mr. Zeller seems to oppose the petition; however, the objection has not been served and not in the proper format. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879.
